EOLR

Zhouheng Sun 2018-2-21 https://docs.google.com/document/d/1dvGERLfN-0rVwN914HH1zRPHLfdM6d5rPfe0HxOMK08

3/1 (UL/UF/UR/DF)

Stacked (UF/DF) M' U M2 U M'

Recognition:

One LR edge is the tip of the arrow and the other is the bad edge on D.

MC Solution:

Do an M move to solve with misoriented centers.

Good (UR/UB, UR/DB, UL/UB, UL/DB) U M' U M

Recognition:

One LR edge is in UL or UR and the other is in UB or DB.

Solution:

Do an M move that puts the bottom bad edge on U. Adjust the top LR edge to be over the bottom LR edge. Do an M move to solve.

Bottom (DF/DB) M' U M U M U M' U

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on D.

Solution:

See Bad 3/1.

MC Solution:

Adjust the tip of the arrow to be to the side. Do an M move that puts the the good LR edge on U. Adjust the tip of the arrow to where you just moved that LR edge to. Undo the M move. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Adjacent (UR/UF, UL/UF) M' U M U M2 U M U2 M U'

Recognition:

Both LR edges are bad and adjacent to each other.

Solution:

See Bad 3/1.

MC Solution:

Adjust the non-tip LR edge to be over the bottom bad edge. Do an M move that puts the bottom bad edge on U. Do a U2. Undo the M move. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Bad (UL/UR, UB/UF, UB/DF, UB/DB, UL/DF, UR/DF) M' U M' U' M U2 M U' M2 U

Recognition:

Not one of the above cases.

Solution:

Do an M move that brings the misoriented D edge to U. Adjust the U face either direction. Do an M move that puts the LR edge currently in the M-slice on D. Solve LR.

4/0 (UL/UF/UR/UB)

Opposite (UL/UR, UF/UB) M' U M2 U M' U2 M U2 M

Recognition:

LR edges are opposite each other on U.

MC Solution:

Adjust the LR edges to be in UF and UB and do an M'. Solve LR with misoriented centers.

Good (UL/DF, UL/DB) U M U' M U2 M U2 M

Recognition:

One LR edge is on U and the other is on D.

Solution:

Move the top LR edge to the side. Do M' U2 M. Good 3/1 from there.

Adjacent (UL/UF) M' U M U' M' U2 M U M' U2 M U

Recognition:

LR edges are adjacent on U.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that puts the M-slice LR edge on D. Do U2 and undo the original M move. Adjacent 3/1 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) U M' U2 M U2 M U M U2 M U2 M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on D.

Solution:

Adjust the corners so the FD sticker matches the corners on L. Do M' U M'. UF Adjacent 1/1 from there.

2o/2 (UL/UR/DF/DB)

Bottom (DF/DB) M' U M2 U M U2 M U2 M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on D.

MC Solution:

Do M' U2 M. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Adjacent (UL/UB, UR/UF) U M' U' M' U2 M U2 M

Recognition:

One LR edge is oriented on top and the other is misoriented on top.

Solution:

Do M U2 M'. Good 3/1 from there.

Decent (UL/DF, UR/DF) U M' U' M' U M U M U' M' U' M

Recognition:

One LR edge is misoriented on U and the other is on D.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that brings the LR edge on bottom to the top. Do a U2. Undo the initial M move. Adjacent 3/1 from there.

Misoriented Opposite (UL/UR) M' U M U2 M U2 M U M2

Recognition:

The LR edges are both misoriented on top.

Solution:

Do an M2. Bottom 2o/2 from there.

Stacked (UF/DF) M' U M' U' M' U' M U M U2 M U2

Recognition:

One LR edge is oriented on U and the other is on D.

MC Solution:

Adjust the top LR edge to be on the side. Do an M move that keeps the bottom LR edge on D, adjust the top LR edge to be over it, and undo the M move. Bottom 2o/2 from there.

Oriented Opposite (UF/UB) U M U M' U M U M U M' U M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are oriented.

Solution:

Do an M', adjust the U face either direction, and undo it with an M. UF Adjacent 1/1 from there.

2a/2 (UL/UB/DF/DB)

Best (UL/UB) M' U M U M2

Recognition:

The LR edges are the misoriented edges on U.

MC Solution:

Do an M2. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Great (UF/DF, UF/DB, UR/DF, UR/DB) U M U' M' U' M2 U

Recognition:

One LR edge is oriented on U and the other is on D.

Solution:

Do an M2. Good 3/1 from there.

Opposite (UL/UR) M' U M' U M' U M' U

Recognition:

The LR edges are opposite each other on U.

MC Solution:

Adjust the LR edges to be in UF and UB. Do an M move that puts the bad LR edge on D. Adjust the other bad edge to be over the bad LR edge. Repeat the initial M move. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Stacked (UL/DF, UL/DB, UB/DF, UB/DB) M' U M' U' M U2 M U2

Recognition:

One LR edge is misoriented on U and the other is on D.

Solution:

Adjust the non-LR bad edge on U to be over the bottom LR edge. Do an M move that keeps the bottom LR edge on bottom, do a U2, and undo the M move. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) U M' U' M U' M U' M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on D.

Solution:

Do an M move that puts one of the bad edges on U onto D. Move the other bad edge on U over it and repeat that M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Mixed Adjacent (UL/UF, UB/UR) U M U' M U' M2 U M U2 M

Recognition:

One LR edge is misoriented on top and the other is adjacent to it and oriented.

Solution:

Adjust the bad LR edge to be in the M-slice. Do an M move that keeps that puts that LR edge on bottom, do a U2, and undo that M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Oriented Adjacent (UF/UR) M' U M' U M' U2 M U2 M U M' U

Recognition:

The LR edges are both oriented.

Solution:

Do an M2. Bad 3/1 from there.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that puts one of the bad edges on U onto D. Move the other bad edge on U over it and repeat that M move. Adjacent 3/1 from there.

1/1 (UF/DB)

Best (UL/UB, UL/DF, UR/DF, UR/DB) U M' U' M' U M U M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are oriented and exactly one is in the M-slice.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that puts the M-slice LR edge on U. Adjust the other LR edge to be across from where you just moved the first one to. Do an M move that puts that same edge on D. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Stacked (UB/DB) U M' U' M' U' M U' M

Recognition:

One LR edge is DB and the other is UB.

MC Solution:

Move the LR edge on U to the side. Do an M move that keeps the other LR edge on D. Solve LR with misoriented centers.

Good (UL/DB, UR/DB) U M U M' U M U M'

Recognition:

One LR edge is DB and the other is not in the M-slice.

Solution:

Do an M move that puts the M-slice LR edge on U. Adjust the other LR to be across from where you just moved the first one to. Undo the M move. Good 3/1 from there.

UF Adjacent (UL/UF, UF/UR) U M U M' U M U M

Recognition:

One LR edge is UF and the other is adjacent to it.

Solution:

Adjust the U layer so the good LR edge is diagonal from the bad edge on D. Do an M move that keeps the good LR edge on top. Adjust the bad LR edge to be where the good LR edge just was. Undo the M move. Good 3/1 from there.

UF Separated (UF/DF, UF/DB) U M' U M' U M U M'

Recognition:

One LR edge is UF and the other is on D.

Solution:

Do an M move that moves puts the top LR edge on D. Adjust the U face either direction. Undo the M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Sides (UL/UR) U M' U' M' U2 M U' M U

Recognition:

The LR edges are in UL and UR.

Solution:

Adjust the top bad edge to be on the side. Do an M move that keeps the bottom bad edge on D. Adjust the top bad edge to be above the bottom bad edge. Undo the M move. Good 3/1 from there.

UF Opposite (UF/UB) M' U M' U' M U' M' U2 M U M' U2

Recognition:

The LR edges are in UF and UB.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that puts the bad LR edge on D. Do a U and undo the M move. Adjacent 3/1 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) M' U M U M' U M U M' U M' U

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on D.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that keeps the bad LR edge on D. Do a U and undo the M move. Great 2a/2 from there.

Bad (UB/DF) M U' M U' M U2 M U' M' U' M U

Recognition:

Both LR edges are oriented on the M slice.

Solution:

Adjust the top bad edge to be on the side. Do an M move that keeps the bottom bad edge on D. Adjust the top bad edge to be above the bottom bad edge. Undo the M move. Adjacent 3/1 from there.

2o/0 (UF/UB)

Best (UL/DF, UR/DF) U M' U M' U' M U M'

Recognition:

One LR edge is on bottom and the other is oriented on top.

MC Solution:

Do an M move to put the bottom LR edge on top. Adjust the other LR edge to replace it, then undo that M move. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Misoriented (UF/UB) U M' U M U' M' U M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are misoriented.

Solution:

Do M' U M. Good 3/1 from there.

Stacked (UF/DF, UF/DB) U M' U' M' U' M U M'

Recognition:

One LR edge is on D and the other is misoriented.

Solution:

Adjust the top LR edge to be over the bottom one. Do an M to put both on D, do a U', and undo the M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Adjacent (UF/UL, UF/UR) U M' U M U M' U' M

Recognition:

One LR edge is on D and the other is misoriented.

Solution:

Do an M move that keeps the bad LR edge on top. Adjust the U face to replace it with the other LR edge. Undo the M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Opposite (UL/UR) M' U M' U M' U2 M U M U M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are oriented on top.

MC Solution:

Adjust the LR edges to be in the M slice. Do M' U2 M. Best 2o/0 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) U' M U' M' U' M' U2 M U' M U' M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on bottom.

MC Solution:

Adjust the bad edges to be in UL and UR. Do M U2 M'. Best 2o/0 from there.

2a/0 (UL/UB)

Best (UL/UF, UB/UR) M' U M' U2 M U' M2 U M U

Recognition:

One LR edge is misoriented and the other is adjacent to it and oriented.

Solution:

Adjust the good LR edge to be in the M-slice. Do an M move that keeps it on U. Move the other LR edge to that new location and repeat the first M move. Good 3/1 from there.

MC Solution:

Adjust the bad LR edge to be in the M-slice. Do an M move to put it on D. Solve LR with misoriented centers.

Stacked (UF/DF, UF/DB, UR/DF, UR/DB) M' U M U' M' U' M' U' M2

Recognition:

One LR edge is on D and the other is oriented on U.

Solution:

Adjust the top LR edge to be in diagonal from the bottom one. Do an M move that keeps it on top. Move it to be opposite of the bad edge still on U, then undo that M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Good (UL/DF, UL/DB, UB/DF, UB/DB) U M2 U M' U' M U2 M U' M

Recognition:

One LR is on D and the other is misoriented on U.

Solution:

Adjust the non-LR bad edge to be diagonal from the LR edge on bottom. Do an M to bring it to the bottom, move the top LR edge over it, and undo that M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) U M' U M' U2 M U M' U

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on the bottom.

Solution:

Do an M move that puts one of the bad edges on D. Adjust the other bad edge to be over it. Undo the original M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Oriented Adjacent (UF/UR) M' U M U M' U M U M U2 M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are oriented on U.

MC Solution:

Do an M move to put one of the LR edges on D, then a U2, then undo that M move. Best 1/1 from there.

Opposite (UL/UR, UF/UB) M' U M U' M' U' M U' M' U' M U

Recognition:

One LR edge is misoriented and the other is opposite of it.

MC Solution:

Adjust the LR edges to both be in the M-slice. Do an M move to put the good LR edge on D. Adjust the non-LR bad edge to be over it and undo the first M move. Best 1/1 from there.

Misoriented Adjacent (UL/UB) M' U M' U M U M' U' M U M U

Recognition:

Both LR edges are misoriented.

Solution:

Do an M2. UF Separated 1/1 from there.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that keeps both LR edges on top. Move the LR edge not in the M-slice to where the other LR edge now is. Undo the original M move. Best 1/1 from there.

0/2 (DF/DB)

Adjacent (UL/UF, UL/UB) M' U M' U' M U M U

Recognition:

The LR edges are on U and adjacent.

MC Solution:

Do an M move that keeps the M-slice LR edge on U. Adjust the other LR to that position. Repeat the initial M move. Stacked 3/1 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) U M U M' U' M U M

Recognition:

Both LR edges are on D.

Solution:

Adjust the corners so the LR stickers are on L or R. Do M', bring the corners that match the DF sticker (now in FU), and repeat the M'. Good 3/1 from there.

Good (UL/DF, UL/DB, UF/DF, UF/DB) U M U' M' U' M U M

Recognition:

One LR edge is on U and the other is on D.

Solution:

Adjust the top LR edge to be on the side. Do an M move that puts the bottom LR edge on top. Adjust the other LR edge to be in that position. Repeat the first M move. Good 3/1 from there.

Opposite (UL/UR) M' U M' U' M U2 M U M' U M

Recognition:

The LR edges are opposite on U.

MC Solution:

Adjust the LR edges to be in UL and UR. Do M' U M. Stacked 2a/2 from there.

4/2 (UL/UF/UR/UB/DF/DB)

Adjacent (UL/UF, UL/DF, UL/DB) M' U M U' M' U' M U2 M U M

Recognition:

The LR edges are adjacent on U or one is on U and the other is on D.

MC Solution:

If one LR edge is on bottom, adjust the U face so that the top LR edge is not in the M slice. Do an M move that puts the M-slice LR edge on D. Adjust the top LR edge to be diagonally opposite the other. Do an M move to keep the top LR edge on top, adjust the U face either direction, then undo that last M move. Solve LR with misoriented centers.

Opposite (UL/UR) U M' U' M' U2 M' U' M U' M' U' M

Recognition:

The LR edges are opposite on U.

Solution:

Adjust the LR edges to both be in the M-slice. Do M' U M'. Good 1/1 from there.

Bottom (DF/DB) U M' U' M' U2 M' U' M U' M' U' M'

Recognition:

The LR edges are both on D.

Solution:

Adjust the corners so the DF sticker matches the F corners. Do M' U M. Good 1/1 from there.
